what:
OpenFn adaptor for Intuit (QuickBooks). The adaptor lets OpenFn workflows read from and write to Intuit QuickBooks Online Accounting API.

Operations haven't been parsed for this adaptor yet — see the Sources tab for vendor API docs. 12 ready-to-paste snippets are grouped as create (9) and search (3). Vendor homepage: https://developer.intuit.com.
connect:
Intuit supports OAuth 2.0 for API access.

The credential schema asks for baseUrl (required) and access_token (required). Point the credential at the base URL that matches your environment: https://quickbooks.api.intuit.com/v3, https://sandbox-quickbooks.api.intuit.com/v3. Auth reference: https://developer.intuit.com/app/developer/qbo/docs/develop/authentication-and-authorization/oauth-2.0.
when:
Reach for this adaptor when your workflow needs to read or write data in Intuit QuickBooks Online Accounting API.

11 triggers are catalogued (0 webhook, 11 polling), including New Account, New Bill, New Customer, New Estimate, and more. See the Triggers tab for the full list. Intuit can push events to OpenFn via webhooks (docs: https://developer.intuit.com/app/developer/qbo/docs/develop/webhooks), so reactive workflows are a good fit.
wire:
A typical wiring pattern is trigger → fetch → transform → write.

Kick the workflow off from a trigger on the Triggers tab (webhook-driven where supported, polling otherwise). Then chain operations from this adaptor's Operations tab to fetch or write data in Intuit. Pass values between steps through state.data — every operation reads from and writes to job state.

To move data between systems, follow this step with another adaptor (e.g. write the fetched records into a downstream FHIR, DHIS2, or database adaptor).
scale:
When running Intuit jobs at volume:

- Prefer Intuit webhooks over polling — they cut latency and rate-limit pressure.
- Paginate list and search calls explicitly; never assume a single response is the full result set.
- Make jobs idempotent by keying on stable external IDs so a replay doesn't create duplicates.
- Surface 4xx/5xx responses with the vendor request ID in your logs, and treat 429s as retries with backoff.

Adapter note: Intuit does not publish an official OpenAPI; entity references are per-resource HTML pages.
best:
- Store Intuit credentials in OpenFn's credential store; never inline keys or tokens in job code.
- Pin @openfn/language-intuit to v1.0.19 in package.json so an upstream change doesn't break a production workflow silently.
- Keep each step small — one logical API call per operation makes failures easier to diagnose and retry.
- Add defensive checks before transforming inbound payloads — vendor responses change between versions.
- Log enough context (resource id, run id, vendor request id) so an incident can be traced end-to-end.
